# Beudox HR Portal - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a single-page application (SPA) built with React and TypeScript, using Vite as the build tool. The application follows a component-based architecture with clear separation of concerns:

- **Frontend Layer**: React components with TypeScript for type safety
- **Data Layer**: Supabase client for database operations and real-time subscriptions
- **Styling Layer**: Tailwind CSS with shadcn/ui design system
- **Routing Layer**: React Router for client-side navigation

## Key Architectural Decisions

### Component Organization
- Flat component structure in `src/components/` with feature-based subdirectories
- Shared UI components in `src/components/ui/` following shadcn/ui patterns
- Page components in `src/pages/` for route-based rendering
- Custom hooks in `src/hooks/` for reusable logic

### State Management
- Local component state with React hooks (useState, useEffect)
- Context providers for global state (authentication, mobile sidebar)
- Supabase real-time subscriptions for live data updates

### Data Flow
- Direct database queries using Supabase client
- Optimistic updates for better UX
- Error handling with user-friendly toast notifications
- Form validation with custom logic

## Core Business Logic

### Authentication & Authorization
- Supabase Auth for user management
- Role-based access control with permission checks
- Mandatory password change flow for new users
- Session management with automatic refresh

### Attendance Management
- Automated attendance tracking with check-in/check-out
- Overtime calculation (regular and holiday)
- Anomaly detection (late arrivals, absences, incomplete records)
- Monthly summary reports with key metrics

### Leave Management
- Multiple leave types with balance tracking
- Approval workflows
- Calendar integration for leave planning
- Automatic balance updates

### Payroll Processing
- Salary structure with allowances and deductions
- Overtime pay calculations
- Payslip generation
- Tax and benefit calculations

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap and root rendering
- `src/App.tsx`: Main app component with routing
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Dashboard/home page

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main layout with navigation
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Sidebar navigation menu
- `src/components/attendance/AttendanceSummary.tsx`: Attendance analytics dashboard
- `src/components/MandatoryPasswordChange.tsx`: Password reset modal

### Utilities and Libraries
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for class merging)
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Permission and role checking functions
- `src/lib/attendance-format.ts`: Time and date formatting for attendance
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave balance and calculation utilities
- `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`: Supabase client configuration

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Vite build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Tailwind CSS customization
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript compiler options
- `supabase/config.toml`: Supabase project configuration

## Database Schema Highlights

### Core Tables
- `employees`: User profiles and employment data
- `attendance_records`: Daily attendance entries
- `leave_requests`: Leave applications and approvals
- `payroll_records`: Salary and payment data
- `companies`: Multi-tenant company information
- `public_holidays`: Holiday calendar management

### Key Relationships
- Employees belong to companies
- Attendance records link to employees and dates
- Leave requests have approval workflows
- Payroll tied to employee salary structures

## Development Workflow

### Build Process
- Vite for fast development and optimized production builds
- TypeScript for compile-time type checking
- ESLint for code quality enforcement
- PostCSS for CSS processing

### Testing Strategy
- Vitest for unit testing React components and utilities
- Playwright for end-to-end testing of user flows
- Component testing with React Testing Library patterns

### Deployment
- Static site generation ready for modern hosting platforms
- Environment-based configuration for different stages
- Optimized bundle splitting and code splitting

## Security Considerations

- Row Level Security (RLS) enabled on all Supabase tables
- Authentication required for all data access
- Input validation on all forms
- Secure password policies enforced
- API keys properly configured and protected

## Performance Optimizations

- Lazy loading of routes and components
- Optimized re-renders with React.memo and useMemo
- Efficient database queries with proper indexing
- Image optimization and CDN usage
- Bundle size monitoring and optimization

## Future Considerations

- Potential migration to Next.js for SSR/SSG capabilities
- Implementation of React Query for advanced caching
- Addition of offline support with service workers
- Expansion to mobile app with React Native
